news.title: Trump says he doesn't want Iran war ceasefire, but is considering 'winding down' military ops
news.summary: President Donald Trump said the Strait of Hormuz, "will have to be guarded and policed, as necessary, by other Nations who use it"

news.title: OpenClaw's ChatGPT moment sparks concern that AI models are becoming commodities
news.summary: OpenClaw is a technology that didn't exist six months ago. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang dedicated a major part of his keynote to OpenClaw.

news.title: Analysis: Trump's unshackled presidency puts him at the center of the economy
news.summary: The president's unprecedented use of executive power has made him a driving force behind oil prices, the Federal Reserve's interest rates, and more.

news.title: GLP-1 drugs are changing how Americans eat. Food companies are racing to catch up
news.summary: The increasing adoption of GLP-1 drugs present both an opportunity and a threat to their businesses. For restaurants and food companies, the increase in the use of the drug could be an opportunity or threat.

news.title: Buffett defends 'Giving Pledge' against Thiel and 'billionaire backlash'
news.summary: Warren Buffett is defending the philanthropic initiative he co-founded with Bill Gates almost 15 years ago. Warren Buffett and Bill Gates are co-founders of the Buffett-Gates Foundation.

news.title: Jensen Huang once said there was ‘no evidence’ Nvidia chips were getting diverted to China. The scandal at Super Micro suggests otherwise.
news.summary: The case highlights how the industry appears to not have taken the issue of chip smuggling seriously, experts say. The chip industry has been accused of not taking the issue seriously.

news.title: ‘This guy has no manners’: My Airbnb guest requested I buy bacon and beer. The $30 bill remains unpaid. Do I insist?
news.summary: “I don’t want him to retaliate with a bad review,” she says. “I’m not going to let him get away with it.”

news.title: Americans are about to get a crash course in the global economy: Higher prices are coming for pineapples, plastic, chocolate and berries
news.summary: Rising costs for fertilizer, feed, packaging and shipping are going to seep into the prices that people see on grocery shelves, experts say.

news.title: Retirees, steel yourselves: Global crises might rattle the markets, but they don’t have to ruin your retirement
news.summary: The economic shock from the Iran conflict can take on outsize importance for those close to or in retirement. The impact on the economy can be felt for years to come.

news.title: Financial markets are responding to the Iran conflict in unexpected ways — leaving some investors puzzled
news.summary: Gold, often a haven during times of stress, has been falling. Meanwhile, stocks are down, but not as much as many expected.
